ps140: You did not leave the bosom of the Father* and came to earth wearing flesh* because of Your mercy, O Christ.* Having suffered and died, You rose on the third day,* You entered through a closed door as All-Powerful One.* Therefore, Thomas touched Your divine side in joy,** and glorified You as the Lord and the Creator.

O Christ, You were held on the Cross with nails,* Your side was pierced by a spear,* You were given gall to drink,* You suffered death,* You were put in a tomb as a dead man.* But as God You crushed the bonds of Hades,* and rose the dead of all ages with Yourself,* and having broken the seals, You showed Yourself to the disciples,** assuring them of Your Resurrection.

Pascha is a true feast,* the mystical Pascha,* the divine Pascha,* the salvific Pascha,* Pascha that leads us to eternal life,* Pascha that takes all sorrow away,* Pascha that bestows the joyful grace onto the disciples,* making Thomas cry out:* You are My Lord and my God,** Who took the kindom of Hades captive!

Glorious wonder!* Doubt bore certain faith!* Thomas said: unless I see, I shall not believe!* By touching His side, he blessed the incarnate Son of God * Who suffered in the flesh,* and he proclaimed the resurrected God,* crying out with joy: My Lord and my God, glory to You!
aposticha: Glorious wonder!* The grass touched fire and was saved!* Thomas placed his hand on the fiery side of Jesus Christ, God!* He was not scorched by this,* his doubt changed to certain faith and he cried fervently:* You are my Master and God,* resurrected from the dead.* O Lord, glory to You!
